title: Serve your first static site with nginx
problem: You have a fresh Amazon Linux VPS. Nothing is running on it. Install nginx, deploy a simple HTML page and make it publicly accessible on port 80. This is the foundation everything else in this track builds on.

setup.description: Check your VPS is ready before starting.
setup.note: First command confirms you are on Amazon Linux. Second command should fail — meaning port 80 is clear and nothing is running yet. Also make sure port 80 is open in your EC2 Security Group before starting.

hints: Amazon Linux uses yum — sudo yum install nginx. After install nginx does not start automatically, you need to start and enable it manually with systemctl
nginx serves files from /usr/share/nginx/html on Amazon Linux — this is different from Ubuntu which uses /var/www/html
If port 80 is not reachable from outside, go to your EC2 console → Security Groups → Inbound Rules → Add Rule → HTTP port 80 → Source 0.0.0.0/0
solution:
1. Update packages:
   sudo yum update -y
   # Always update before installing anything.
2. Install nginx:
   sudo yum install nginx -y
   # Pulls nginx from the Amazon Linux yum repos.
3. Start nginx:
   sudo systemctl start nginx
   # Starts the service immediately.
4. Enable on boot:
   sudo systemctl enable nginx
   # Ensures nginx restarts automatically after a reboot.
5. Check status:
   sudo systemctl status nginx
   # Confirm it shows 'active (running)'.
6. Test locally:
   curl -I http://localhost
   # Should return HTTP/1.1 200 OK.
7. Create your page:
   sudo nano /usr/share/nginx/html/index.html
   # Amazon Linux serves from here — Ubuntu uses /var/www/html.
8. Set permissions:
   sudo chmod 644 /usr/share/nginx/html/index.html
   # nginx needs read access to serve the file.
9. Reload nginx:
   sudo systemctl reload nginx
   # Applies config/content changes without dropping connections.
10. Test from outside:
   curl -I http://YOUR_EC2_IP
   # Run this from your local machine or browser.
11. If not reachable:
   # EC2 Console → Security Groups → Inbound Rules → Add Rule → HTTP port 80 → Source 0.0.0.0/0
